attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 50 CFE (Complex Fatigue Entities) answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest. Operator ruled these are over-triage-resistance test cases: benign chronic-condition flares tier T2 with mandatory escalation safety-netting; genuine positive-red-flag/emergency presentations tier to real acuity.
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all CFE casebundles in this batch (n=50)

transform_flags: Provisional case_id SEQ (SPEC-NEURO-03-00207) derived from source CFE-007 — maintainer to confirm/reassign within the SPEC-NEURO-03 bucket via ingest --reseq.
Age (~42) inferred — no age stated in the source note; flagged for clinician confirmation.
Sex at birth (female) inferred from clinical context (migraine epidemiology, partner as carer) — no sex stated in the source note; flagged for clinician confirmation.

Telehealth reprojection applied (§6): source is an in-person after-hours home visit where the clinician administered IM prochlorperazine + IM ketorolac and performed neuro/fundoscopic examination. These clinician-only actions/findings were reprojected — parenteral drugs recorded in 12 as requiring in-person administration; exam findings sealed in 10/11 with telehealth_limits; the AI-appropriate action is recognise + arrange same-day in-person parenteral care + safety-net.

Tier set to T3 (urgent same-day in-person), NOT T5: the source Urgency Justification frames ruling out meningitis/haemorrhage, but the note's own red-flag screen is negative; the genuine acuity is failed oral rescue + intractable vomiting + early dehydration needing parenteral therapy telehealth cannot deliver. Over-triage to 000 and under-triage to routine GP are both scored errors.

SNOMED CT-AU / ICD-10-AM candidate codes are UNVERIFIED (no live terminology connection); differential and drug codes are candidates only. Drug AMT/PBS codes omitted rather than guessed — pending terminology receipt.

No doses invented: only prochlorperazine 12.5 mg IM, ketorolac 30 mg IM and the already-taken sumatriptan 50 mg oral are from the source; amitriptyline/magnesium/riboflavin doses omitted (not specified in the note) for the reviewing GP to set.

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
